Each color gets its own waveform + 4-note phrase (Red sawtooth bold G5-E5-G5-C5, Blue sine mellow C5-E5-G5-E5, Green triangle pastoral D5-G5-A5-E5, Yellow sine chirpy A5-C6-E6-C6, Purple triangle minor-tinged A4-D5-F5-A5). Cycle 3 Build Day 4: Replay Sharing 🎬 — new v3 share-link format encodes a saved Track Replay (Day 47) into the URL hash. Friends opening the link see the baseline state, then watch the ghost auto-rebuild the track after a 1.5s beat. v3 layout: [version=3, rows, cols, grid (1 byte/cell), trains, switches, 2-byte action count, per-action bytes] where each action is 1-byte kind + 0-3 bytes payload (place=3, remove/rotate=2, placeTrain=3, clearAll=0). Backward-compatible: v1 (legacy 12x8) and v2 (rows/cols) links keep working untouched, decoders branch on the version byte. Share modal gains a third button 🎬 Replay Link beside 🔗 Copy Link and 📸 Save Image; guarded against empty/recording state with friendly toasts. Decoder side-channels via pendingReplayFromShare flag that init() consumes to trigger playReplay() after a 1.5s delay so the visitor sees the baseline first. Round-trip test: 8-action replay = 178 char base64 URL, decodes to same actions + baseline. +160 LOC (11,497→11,656, 412 KB), 0 bugs, JS clean, v2 backward compat verified live on deploy.

Cycle 3 Build Day 5: Sticker Book ⭐ — 12 collectible milestone stickers in a 4×3 modal grid. Earned stickers glow in warm gold/cream with a pop animation; unearned are grayed silhouettes with ❓. Sticker set: 🚂 First Steps (run 1 train), 🔨 Builder (25 tracks), 🏗️ Master Builder (100 tracks), 🔁 Loop Maker (10 loops), 🧩 Puzzler (3 puzzles), 🌟 Puzzle Star (10 puzzles), 📦 Delivery Pro (10 cargo), 🐄 Animal Friend (5 animals), 🎨 Decorator (25 scenery), 🌙 Night Owl (toggle night), 🎵 DJ (try all 3 sound packs), ⭐ Train Master (meta — every other sticker earned). State lives in localStorage as `trainTracks_stickers` ({earned, soundPacksTried, nightToggled}). Hooks: incrementStat() re-evaluates after every stat change, setSoundPack() records each distinct pack tried, toggleNightMode() flips the Night Owl flag. init() seeds the current pack + night-mode flag so existing players retroactively earn what they've already done. Award path uses two passes (regular stickers first, then meta) and a staggered toast queue (1.4s apart) with SFX.celebrate per sticker. Modal opens from Settings menu (⭐ Sticker Book). Verified live: 1/12 baseline render → simulated stats → 12/12 with Train Master correctly gated last. +217 LOC (11,656→11,873, 413 KB), 0 bugs, JS clean, 0 console errors.

Cycle 4 Build Day 1: 🦋 Ambient Critters — small butterflies + bees flit above nature scenery (flower/tree/sheep/cow/horse/people/house/duck-land) while a train is playing. Pure CSS wander animation with 3 variant keyframes (crit-wander-a/b/c) and randomized duration (4-7s) + negative delay so each critter has its own phase. JS picks up to 6 random scenery anchors via Fisher-Yates shuffle, positions critters above each anchor with small jitter (0.4*cell horizontally, 0.2*cell vertically), spawns them in startPlay() right after the sky cycle, and tears them down via cleanupAmbientCritters() in stopPlay(). Bee 🐝 over tree/house anchors (hive vibes); butterfly 🦋 over everything else. Auto-on (zero new toolbar surface). Respects prefers-reduced-motion (animation: none). animations-paused class also pauses critters for consistency. Verified live on ?v=74&fresh=1: 35 scenery anchors on first random track, 0 critters before play, 6 critters during play (5🦋 + 1🐝), all with computed animationName matching crit-wander-{a,b,c} and unique durations, 0 critters after stopPlay. Screenshot confirms blue butterflies on cow + sunflowers + horse, bee on house. 11,790 → 11,882 LOC (+92), 419,531 → 423,635 bytes (+4,104). 0 bugs, 0 console errors, JS parse clean.

Cycle 4 Build Day 2: 🚦 Station Arrival Signal — every station now wears a tiny 3-dot stoplight (red/yellow/green) in its upper-right corner during play. Green when no train is incoming, yellow when a train is ~2 cells out along the connected rail, red when a train is 0-1 cells from arrival. State driven by `data-state` on the wrapper; CSS picks which dot lights up. Detection uses BFS up to depth 3 along the rail (via existing getConnections/neighborConnected predicates), so a train on a parallel siding correctly stays green. Throttled to ~8 Hz (120ms gate) — perf trivial even on 60 fps animateFrame. Wired into startPlay (setupStationSignals after spawnAmbientCritters), animateFrame (updateStationSignals(timestamp) right after updateCrossingGates), and stopPlay (cleanupStationSignals after cleanupAmbientCritters). Visual: tiny dark-translucent housing, 4×4px dots, lit dot uses background + matching box-shadow glow (red/yellow blink at 0.6s/1.2s, green steady — mirrors real-world stoplight psychology). Respects prefers-reduced-motion (no animation, just transitions) and high-contrast (housing forced black with white outline + dots bumped to 5px). Verified live on ?v=75&fresh=1: 2 stations spawned by random track → 2 signals at play start → over 8 seconds, state distribution {green:121, yellow:16, red:23} matches train traversal pattern → stopPlay cleans 0 signals → re-start cleanly spawns 2 again. 0 bugs, 0 console errors, JS parse clean. 11,882 → 12,007 LOC (+125), 423,635 → 428,940 bytes (+5,305).

Cycle 4 Build Day 4: 🌧️ Puddle Splashes — small blue puddles randomly form on horizontal track tiles (E↔W passable: straight @ 90/270, crossover, bridge, horizontal tunnel/station) when weather is rain AND state.playing. When a train rolls into a puddled cell, 4 droplets fan upward (dx -18→+18, dy -16→-30, dur 0.45–0.7s) with a “kssh” SFX (180ms highpass-noise burst + 1400→420Hz sine sweep). MAX_PUDDLES=6, spawn interval 2.2s @ 55% chance, capped via puddleEls Map keyed by ‘r,c’. Activation: startPlay() fires startPuddleSystem() if currentWeather===‘rain’; mid-play applyWeather() rain→non-rain calls stopPuddleSystem(5000) so puddles fade gracefully (CSS .puddle.fading class), non-rain→rain re-activates; stopPlay() always tears down immediately. Splash trigger is a one-liner in advanceTrainAnim right after anim.row/col update (cheap Map.has guard makes it safe on every move). Pure CSS animations for form/shimmer/fade/droplet-fly with prefers-reduced-motion + night-mode + high-contrast variants. Eager first spawn at 600ms inside startPuddleSystem() so kids see a puddle without waiting for the full interval. Verified live on ?v=77&fresh=1: 8 horizontal cells detected on first random track, puddle spawn cadence 0→1→2 over 4.5s of play, manual triggerPuddleSplash spawned 4 droplets w/ correct anchored CSS vars, rain→sunny faded 6→6 (with .fading class)→0 across the 5s window, rain→sunny→rain re-spawned cleanly, stopPlay zeroed interval+timeout+DOM. 12,122 → 12,388 LOC (+266), ~428,940 → 444,163 bytes. 0 bugs, 0 console errors, JS parse clean.

Cycle 4 Build Day 5: 🛤️ Train Trail — each train drops a faint color-matched fading dot at its visual center every 14 px of travel. Dot inherits TRAIN_COLORS[color].body for both fill and matching double box-shadow (6px outer + 2px inner) → reads as soft glow. CSS train-trail-fade keyframes animate opacity 0.55→0 over 1.5s with parallel scale 1→0.4 shrink; body.night-mode swaps to train-trail-fade-night (opacity 0.75→0) so dots pop against the dark sky. Each dot self-destructs via setTimeout(remove, 1600ms) (CSS animation + 100ms safety margin) so DOM never accumulates. Spawn gate is squared-distance (dx²+dy² >= 196) so trail spacing is tied to actual path length, not wall-clock time — same spacing at any speed. Skipped inside tunnels (loco is fading there), under prefers-reduced-motion, and once the train is crashing/finished. z-index 44 sits below the loco (z=50) so the train rolls cleanly over its own trail. stopPlay's existing .crash-star/.crash-dust/.rainbow-sparkle sweep extended to include .train-trail-dot for instant teardown. Hooked into renderTrainAtProgress one line after anim.trainEl.style.top = (locoY + bob) so spawn position uses the same translate(-50%,-50%) shared visual center as the SVG. Verified live on ?v=78&fresh=1&cb=trail2: single red train → 10-11 dots steady-state with bg rgb(229,57,53) and computed animationName=train-trail-fade; added 2nd blue train mid-play → 11 dots split {red:6, blue:5}; toggled body.night-mode mid-play → all live dots switched to train-trail-fade-night instantly with no JS path; stopPlay() zeroed 9 live dots in one tick. 12,388 → 12,481 LOC (+93, well within roadmap +90-130 estimate), 444,163 → 448,310 bytes (+4,147). 0 bugs, 0 console errors, JS parse clean. Cycle 4 Build week closes at +691 LOC across 5 features (Critters, Station Signal, Confetti Cannon, Puddle Splashes, Train Trail) — under the +900 advisory budget by 209 lines.

Prior: Cycle 2 Build Week, Day 5 (Day 48, May 7): 🔊 SOUND PACKS shipped — Cycle 2 Build Week is COMPLETE. Roadmap item from cycle-2-build.md (final day). Three timbral palettes for the train’s whistle, chug rhythm, station horn, and HONK button: 🚢 Classic (current sounds, 880Hz sine whistle, 800Hz bandpass chug, triangle horn), 🧸 Toy Steamer (1320Hz sine whistle, 1400Hz softer chug, sine horn at 1.6× pitch, brighter noise hiss), 🚆 Modern Diesel (392Hz sawtooth whistle, 360Hz low rumble chug, sawtooth horn at 0.7× pitch, gritty 1200Hz noise burst). Architecture: new `SOUND_PACKS = {classic, toy, diesel}` config object near the audio engine, each pack contains parameters for whistle (f1/f2/type/vol/dur/delay), chug (filterFreq/filterQ/vol + accentLow/High/VolLow/VolHigh/Type), horn (mul/mainType/harmonicMul/harmonicType/noiseFreq/noiseVol), and station (tone1Type/tone2Type/overtoneType/overtoneVol). The SFX functions themselves stay shared and just call `getSoundPack().whistle.f1` etc. — zero duplicated synthesis code. Modified four playback paths: SFX.whistle (start of play 2-tone), SFX.station (3-oscillator station horn), playChugWithEcho + startChugLoop accent (chug rhythm during play), playHornSound (HONK button per-color horn). Per-pack pitch shifts on horn use a multiplier on HORN_PITCHES so the per-color (red/blue/green/yellow/purple) variation is preserved — toy steamer = bright high-pitched sine across all 5 colors, diesel = bass-y sawtooth across all 5. New Settings entry 🔊 'Sound: Classic/Toy/Diesel' is a CYCLE button (not a dropdown) — click rolls Classic→Toy→Diesel→Classic with a tiny preview whistle on each change so kids hear the difference instantly; toast confirms `🚢 Sound Pack: Classic`. Persistence via new localStorage key `trainTracks_soundPack` (just the name string), restored in init() right after restoreAudioSettings(); restoreSoundPack() validates the key against SOUND_PACKS so corrupt LS falls back to classic. Prior: Cycle 2 Build Week, Day 4 (Day 47, May 6): 👻 TRACK REPLAY / GHOST TRAIN shipped. Roadmap item from cycle-2-build.md. New Settings entry '👻 Track Replay' opens a modal with three buttons: 🔴 Record / ⏹️ Stop, ▶️ Replay, 🗑️ Clear. Implementation: state gains `replayRecording` (bool), `replayActions` (array of `{kind:'place'|'remove'|'rotate'|'placeTrain'|'clearAll', row, col, type?, rotation?, color?}` records), `replayBaseline` (snapshot of grid+trains+switchStates+bigGrid taken at REC start). Recording hooks installed inside `placePiece`, `removePiece`, `rotatePiece`, `placeTrain`, `clearAll` — each calls `logReplayAction(…)` at the top, which appends to the action log when `state.replayRecording && !replayApplying`. Replay flow: stop play if running, restore baseline silently (replayApplying=true so action hooks don't re-record), re-call the same low-level functions on a fixed 600ms cadence (200ms when prefers-reduced-motion), update `👻 Replaying • k/N` indicator each step, then 500ms after the last step `startPlay()` auto-runs the train if any are placed. Cancel handling via incrementing `replayCancelTokenId` (new `playReplay()` invalidates earlier in-flight runs). Ambient REC/ghost indicator pill (top-center) toggles between red `REC • N steps` while recording and purple `👻 Replaying • k/N` during playback, with pulsing dot animation that respects prefers-reduced-motion. UI lock during replay via `body.replay-active` CSS rule (pointer-events:none on #grid, #sidebar, #drawer-content, #toolbar, #trash-zone, plus filter:saturate(0.85) on #grid for visual ghost feel). Persistence: separate localStorage key `trainTracks_replay` stores `{baseline, actions}` JSON — ~880 bytes for a 5-step recording. Restored in `init()` after `loadGameStats/loadUnlockState/etc`. Settings menu's Track Replay label dynamically shows '🔴 Recording (N)' / 'Replay (N steps)' / 'Track Replay' depending on state. Esc closes the new modal; outside-click closes; modal is blocked while a puzzle is active. Live-tested on https://mikedyan.github.io/train-tracks/?v=47: clean LS → startReplayRecording() captures empty baseline + replayRecording=true + indicator visible ✓; placed 4 curves + 1 train via direct calls → 5 actions logged with correct kinds (4 place + 1 placeTrain) ✓; stopReplayRecording() → saved=true, savedActions=5 in LS, indicator hidden ✓; cleared board (4→0 pieces) → playReplay() → step probes at +1s/+3.5s/+5s confirmed: 1s in 2 pieces placed, 3.5s in 4 pieces + 1 train, 5s in playing=true + animTrains=1 (auto-startPlay fired ~3.5–4s after replay end) ✓; reload page → state.replayActions=5, baselinePresent=true, baselineGridLen=8 (12-col grid, 8 rows) ✓; modal status 'Saved replay: 5 steps', Replay button enabled, Settings label 'Replay (5 steps)' ✓; clearReplay() → actions=0, baseline=null, LS removed, modal back to 'No replay saved yet' ✓. Zero console errors on deployed site. JS parses cleanly via `new Function(js)` syntax check on the inlined script tag (293,273 bytes). Code health: 10,734 → 11,099 lines (+365 net for full feature: ~85 lines CSS for modal + indicator + grid lock, ~16 lines HTML for modal/indicator markup + Settings entry, ~265 lines JS for engine + hooks + persistence + UI helpers). Acceptance from roadmap: ✓ build a 6-piece loop with REC on (4-curve loop + train tested, system handles 5+ actions cleanly), ✓ press Replay → ghost rebuilds piece-by-piece, ✓ train auto-runs at end. KNOWN LIMITATIONS: (1) replay is NOT shared via URL hash — share links still encode static layout only; (2) actions reference absolute grid coords so a replay recorded on Big Grid (16×10) and replayed after toggling to small grid will silently drop out-of-bounds steps (`placePiece` / `placeTrain` early-return on invalid coords); (3) headless/unfocused tab throttling makes the 500ms auto-startPlay delay measure ~1.5–3s in CDP smoke tests — functionally fine for kids on a focused tab.
